pattern: The determination of whether or not an incident has taken place where interest was expressed or advice was given by a medical health professional or any individual to reduce or modify existing activity connected with the drinking of beverages or other liquids containing ethanol or other alcohols as reported by a person receiving medical care or treatment.
replace_with: Indicates whether a patient has received a recommendation from a health professional or other individual to reduce or modify alcohol consumption behavior.

pattern: Assessment of a newborn which is recorded at five minutes from the time of birth and expressed as a number quantifying the overall physical condition, which includes heart rate, muscle tone, respiratory effort, color, and reflex responsiveness. This score describes the participant's/subject's child. Activity pulse grimace appearance respiration (APGAR)
replace_with: APGAR assessment of a newborn recorded at five minutes after birth, evaluating overall physical condition including heart rate, muscle tone, respiratory effort, color, and reflex responsiveness.

pattern: Assessment of a newborn which is recorded at ten minutes from the time of birth and expressed as a number quantifying the overall physical condition, which includes heart rate, muscle tone, respiratory effort, color, and reflex responsiveness. This score describes the participant's/subject's child. Activity pulse grimace appearance respiration (APGAR)
replace_with: APGAR assessment of a newborn recorded at ten minutes after birth, evaluating overall physical condition including heart rate, muscle tone, respiratory effort, color, and reflex responsiveness.

pattern: The critical congenital heart defects (CCHD) newborn screening interpretation is the end result of the screening event and is based on the preductal and postductal oxygen saturation measurements as well as the difference between the preductal and postductal measurement. The interpretation will also depend on the particular protocol used for screening.
replace_with: Interpretation of critical congenital heart defect newborn screening based on preductal and postductal oxygen saturation measurements and the difference between them.

pattern: Common high-risk HPV genotypes associated with cervical neoplasia can be detected by genomic probes. This code is based on, but not limited in use to, the submitter's assay INFORM HPV III Family 16 Probe (B), which contains a cocktail of HPV genomic probes targeting DNA from 12 high-risk genotypes (16, 18, 31, 33, 35, 39, 45, 51, 52, 56, 58, and 66).
replace_with: Laboratory detection of twelve high-risk human papillomavirus genotypes (16, 18, 31, 33, 35, 39, 45, 51, 52, 56, 58, and 66) in tissue specimens using genomic DNA probes for cervical neoplasia assessment.

pattern: The semi-quantitative description for rate of occurrence of an event for a perceived need or craving at a time the past calendar year to consume a drink containing alcohol when waking in the morning after a prior day drinking beverages or other liquids containing ethanol or other alcohols as reported by a person receiving medical care or treatment.
replace_with: Self-reported frequency of needing an alcoholic drink in the morning after a prior day of drinking, assessed over the past calendar year.

pattern: the yes/no indicator that asks whether the participant had used benzodiazepines, which includes misuse of anxiety medications, such as diazepam (Valium), lorazepam (Ativan), clonazepam (Klonopin), chlordiazepoxide (Librium), oxazepam (Serepax), and alprazolam (Xanax) as part of the Timeline Followback Method assessment, a calendar-based measure for assessing self-reported retrospective daily estimates of alcohol, tobacco and/or illicit substance use.
replace_with: Self-reported assessment of benzodiazepine use, including misuse of prescription anxiety medications such as diazepam, lorazepam, clonazepam, and alprazolam, using a calendar-based retrospective recall method.

pattern: The calculated value or added sum resulting from semi-quantitative responses from 10 questions on the validated instrument named the Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test (AUDIT, either self or interviewer administered) to assess potential risks, including substance dependence, from behavior of an individual related to consumption of liquids containing ethanol or other alcohols.
replace_with: Calculated total score from the Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test (AUDIT), which may be self- or interviewer-administered, assessing potential risks and substance dependence related to alcohol consumption.

pattern: An indication of immobilization for 3 or more consecutive days or surgery in the previous 4 weeks, according to Wells Criteria. Code 1.5 if experienced immobilization for 3 or more consecutive days or surgery in the previous 4 weeks, and code 0 if there was no immobilization for 3 or more consecutive days or surgery in the previous 4 weeks.
replace_with: Indicates whether a patient has experienced immobilization for three or more consecutive days or undergone surgery within the previous four weeks, as part of clinical risk stratification for pulmonary embolism.

pattern: The XXX system is for all the other things such as syringes, needles, pills, powder, pixie sticks. It is a true unknown found in or on a straw, syringe etc that emergency staff need to know if it is a drug so they can treat the patient. The pills and powder are tested by mass spec to identify substance to help with diagnosis and treatment.
replace_with: Laboratory identification of an unknown substance found in a specimen, using mass spectrometry to assist emergency diagnosis and treatment.

pattern: The date and time at which the patient was last known to be without the signs and symptoms of the current stroke or at his or her prior baseline. This element captures the date and time for last known well that is determined to be the most accurate after all the data have been evaluated and additional witnesses interviewed (if applicable).
replace_with: Records the date and time a stroke patient was last known to be at neurological baseline or free of stroke symptoms, determined after evaluating all available data and witness accounts.

pattern: The XXX system is for all the other things such as syringes, needles, pills, powder, pixie sticks. It is a true unknown found in or on a straw, syringe etc that emergency staff need to know if it is a drug so they can treat the patient. The pills and powder are tested by mass spec to identify substance to help with diagnosis and treatment.
replace_with: Laboratory identification of nicotine in a specimen, using mass spectrometry to assist with substance identification for emergency diagnosis and treatment.

pattern: The determination of whether or not an incident has taken place where damage to the self or another individual is inflicted on the body as a direct or indirect result connected with behavior involving the drinking of beverages or other liquids containing ethanol or other alcohols as reported by a person receiving medical care or treatment.
replace_with: Self-reported indicator of whether a patient has sustained injury to themselves or another person as a direct or indirect result of alcohol consumption.

pattern: Zip code for patient's current residence; String of characters used to identify the five-digit Zone Improvement Plan (ZIP) code and the four-digit extension code (if available) that represents the geographic segment that is a subunti of the ZIP code assigned by the U.S. Postal Service to a geographic location to facilitate mail delivery.
replace_with: Records the patient's residential ZIP code, using the five-digit Zone Improvement Plan code with an optional four-digit extension for more precise geographic identification.

pattern: High sensitivity measurement of CRP may be used to assess the risk of future coronary heart disease, and as an additional independent indicator of recurrent event prognosis in patients with stable coronary disease or acute coronary disease. This term was created for, but not limited in use to, the cobas Cardiac C-Reactive Protein (Latex) High Sensitive test kit.
replace_with: High-sensitivity measurement of C-reactive protein in serum or plasma, used to assess risk of coronary heart disease and as a prognostic indicator for recurrent cardiovascular events.

pattern: 3-Hydroxyisovalerylcarnitine/Octanoylcarnitine is a primary marker for 3-Methylcrotonyl-CoA carboxylase deficiency, Beta-Ketothiolase deficiency, Multiple carboxylase deficiency, 3-Hydroxy-3-methylglutaric aciduria, 2-Methyl-3-hydroxybutyric aciduria, Maternal 3-Methylcrotonyl-CoA carboxylase deficiency, and 2-Methylbutyrylglycinuria.
replace_with: Newborn screening marker ratio of 3-hydroxyisovalerylcarnitine to octanoylcarnitine, used to detect inborn errors of metabolism including 3-methylcrotonyl-CoA carboxylase deficiency, beta-ketothiolase deficiency, and multiple carboxylase deficiency.

pattern: The semi-quantitative description for rate of occurrence of a distinct event where a loss of memory caused by organic and/or psychological factors is experienced following an occurrence of drinking beverages or other liquids containing ethanol or other alcohols calendar year as reported by a person receiving medical care or treatment.
replace_with: Self-reported frequency of alcohol-related memory loss episodes over the past calendar year.

pattern: The date and time that a new* pharmacotherapeutic treatment was initiated. If the time is not available or not applicable, code only the date. *New pharmacotherapeutic treatment is defined as the initiation of any pharmacotherapeutic treatment, or a change in an existing treatment such as change in dose, duration, frequency, or brand.
replace_with: Records the date and time a new or modified pharmacotherapeutic treatment was initiated, including changes in dose, duration, frequency, or brand.

pattern: Skin pigmentation scored 0 to 3 points according to VCSS criteria: none or focal, low intensity (tan; 0 points), diffuse, but limited in area and old (brown; 1 point), diffuse over most of gaiter distribution (lower 1/3) or recent pigmentation (purple; 2 points), wider distribution (above lower 1/3) and recent pigmentation (3 points).
replace_with: Assessment of skin pigmentation severity in venous disease, graded by intensity, distribution, and recency of pigmentation changes in the lower extremity.

pattern: The status related to glycogen storage pathology which involves excess PAS+ material that is easily identifiable on light microscopy and is evident on other stains including H and E. Mild glycogen storage pathology is difficult to determine at the light microscopic level and may only be evident on PAS stain or electron microscopy.
replace_with: Histopathological assessment of excessive glycogen storage in biopsy or autopsy tissue, identified through light microscopy and special stains including PAS and H&E.

pattern: This term is used to report the bone age (e.g. in years, months), which can be computed by various x-ray methods. To report which method was used to determine bone age, use the Bone age method term [LOINC: 85150-1]. For mapping to an X-ray procedure for determining bone age, see the Views bone age XR terms (e.g. [LOINC: 24724-7]).
replace_with: Radiographic assessment of skeletal maturity expressed as bone age, determined by x-ray evaluation of bone development.

pattern: Date of initial diagnosis by a recognized medical practitioner for the tumor being reported whether clinically or microscopically confirmed. See page 81 for date format. For more discussion on determining date of diagnosis, consult the SEER Program Manual or COC FORDS Manual... NAACCR Data Standards and Data Dictionary Version 11
replace_with: Records the date of initial cancer diagnosis by a recognized medical practitioner, whether determined clinically or by microscopic confirmation.

pattern: Measurement of the difference between end-diastolic and end-systolic dimensions of the left ventricle divided by end-diastolic dimension assessed with echocardiography. FS or Fractional Shortening in percentage = EDD minus ESD divided by EDD times 100, where EDD = LV End Diastolic Dimension and ESD = LV End Systolic Dimension.
replace_with: Echocardiographic measurement of left ventricular fractional shortening, reflecting the difference between end-diastolic and end-systolic dimensions relative to end-diastolic dimension.

pattern: Measurement of the difference between end-diastolic and end-systolic dimensions of the right ventricle divided by end-diastolic dimension assessed with echocardiography. FS or Fractional Shortening in percentage = EDD minus ESD divided by EDD times 100, where EDD = RV End Diastolic Dimension and ESD = RV End Systolic Dimension
replace_with: Echocardiographic measurement of right ventricular fractional shortening, reflecting the difference between end-diastolic and end-systolic dimensions relative to end-diastolic dimension.

pattern: If birthplace of mother is captured, birthplace of father, birthplace of maternal grandmother, birthplace of maternal grandmother, birthplace of Paternal grandmother, birthplace of maternal grandfather, birthplace of Paternal grandfather should also be captured to provide additional information about the respondent's ancestry.
replace_with: Records the birthplace of the participant's mother, collected alongside paternal and grandparental birthplace data to characterize ancestry.

pattern: The signal quality indicates the strength or reliability of the measurement at a point in time. Postductal signal quality indicates that the pulse oximetry device is placed in an anatomical location whose blood supply originates from the aorta after the junction of the aorta with the ductus arteriosus (usually the right hand).
replace_with: Signal quality indicator for postductal pulse oximetry, reflecting the strength and reliability of the oxygen saturation measurement taken from a location distal to the ductus arteriosus.

pattern: The signal quality indicates the strength or reliability of the measurement at a point in time. Preductal signal quality indicates that the pulse oximetry device is placed in an anatomical location whose blood supply originates from the aorta before the junction of the aorta with the ductus arteriosus (usually the right hand).
replace_with: Signal quality indicator for preductal pulse oximetry, reflecting the strength and reliability of the oxygen saturation measurement taken from a location proximal to the ductus arteriosus.

pattern: The maximum attenuation score for "spot sign" on computed tomography (CT) angiography to identify patients with intracerebral hemorrhage (ICH) who are at high risk for hematoma expansion. Intracerebral hemorrhage (ICH), a CT angiography spot sign (i.e., active contrast extravasation) indicates a high risk of hematoma expansion
replace_with: CT angiography assessment of spot sign, indicating active contrast extravasation in patients with intracerebral hemorrhage to identify those at high risk for hematoma expansion.

pattern: A multi-disciplinary field of study devoted to gaining greater scientific knowledge about the influence of health determinants, and translating this knowledge into interventions to reduce or eliminate adverse health differences affects disadvantaged populations, based on one or more of the following health outcomes. C164027
replace_with: Captures health disparity outcomes at the project level, documenting the influence of health determinants on adverse health differences affecting disadvantaged populations.

pattern: Note to interviewer: Respondents who were classified as a "Current Every-Day Smoker" by answering the Tobacco - Smoking Status adult Question 1 as ""Yes"" (Have you smoked at least 100 cigarettes in your entire life?) and Question 2 as ""Every day"" (Do you now smoke cigarettes every day, some days, or not at all?) are asked.
replace_with: Self-reported age at which a current every-day cigarette smoker first began smoking on a daily basis.

pattern: The thought process or justification for an action or for not performing an action (encounter, intervention or procedure, laboratory test, diagnostic study, physical exam, device order, medication administration, etc.). Examples include patient, system, or medical-related reasons for declining to perform specific actions.
replace_with: Documents the clinical rationale for performing or not performing a healthcare action, such as an encounter, intervention, procedure, laboratory test, or medication administration.

pattern: Severe lipid storage pathology involves excess Oil Red O+ material that is easily identifiable on light microscopy and is evident on other stains including H and E. Mild lipid storage pathology is difficult to determine at the light microscopic level and may only be evident on Oil Red O stain or on electron microscopy.
replace_with: Histopathological grading of excessive intracellular lipid storage severity in biopsy or autopsy tissue, assessed using Oil Red O stain, H&E, and electron microscopy.

pattern: Midpoint of the distance from the acromion process of the shoulder to the tip of the olecranon process of the mid-elbow. First, the upper arm length is measured using bone landmarks, and the midpoint distance is marked for the circumference measurement. Measure the right arm at the level of the upper arm midpoint mark.
replace_with: Anthropometric measurement of right mid-upper arm circumference, taken at the midpoint between the acromion process and the olecranon process.

pattern: The semi-quantitative description for rate of occurrence of an event for perceived loss of control or inability to self-regulate or stop initiated behavior regarding the drinking of beverages or other liquids containing ethanol or other alcohols calendar year as reported by a person receiving medical care or treatment.
replace_with: Self-reported frequency of being unable to stop or control alcohol drinking behavior over the past calendar year.

pattern: Infant activity during postductal oxygen saturation measurement describes how active the infant was during the measurement. If an infant is crying or in a deep sleep, the oxygen saturation measurement may not be accurate; therefore, knowing the infant's state is important for interpreting the oxygen saturation result.
replace_with: Records the infant's activity state during postductal oxygen saturation measurement, as crying or deep sleep may affect the accuracy of the reading.

pattern: A nine-digit number issued to U.S. citizens, permanent residents, and temporary (working) residents under section 205(c)(2) of the Social Security Act, codified as 42 U.S.C. ? 405(c)(2). The number is issued to an individual by the Social Security Administration, an independent agency of the United States government.
replace_with: Records a patient's Social Security number, the nine-digit identifier issued by the Social Security Administration to U.S. citizens and eligible residents.

pattern: Note to interviewer: Respondents who were classified as a "Former Smoker" by answering the Tobacco - Smoking Status adult protocol, Question 1 as ""Yes"" (Have you smoked at least 100 cigarettes in your entire life?) and Question 2 as ""Not at all"" (Do you now smoke every day, some days or not at all?) are asked.
replace_with: Self-reported duration since a former smoker completely quit smoking cigarettes, asked of individuals who have smoked at least 100 cigarettes in their lifetime but no longer smoke.

pattern: Status of the examination of the chest, part of the human body between the neck and the diaphragm, or abdomen, the area of the body that contains the pancreas, stomach, intestine, liver, gallbladder, and other organs, for diagnostic purposes by means of roentgen rays, recording the image on a sensitized surface.
replace_with: Chest X-ray examination for diagnostic purposes, evaluating the thoracic region between the neck and diaphragm using radiographic imaging.

pattern: For patients who have smoked at least one cigarette with year (Joint Commission definition) or who vape, indicates whether the patient received counseling to stop smoking/vaping or smoking cessation advice during the hospitalization as documented in progress notes or physician orders at discharge or admissions.
replace_with: Indicates whether a patient who smokes or vapes received smoking or vaping cessation counseling or advice during hospitalization, as documented in clinical notes or discharge orders.

pattern: Severe glycogen storage pathology involves excess PAS+ material that is easily identifiable on light microscopy and is evident on other stains including H and E. Mild glycogen storage pathology is difficult to determine at the light microscopic level and may only be evident on PAS stain or electron microscopy.
replace_with: Histopathological grading of glycogen storage severity in biopsy or autopsy tissue, ranging from mild (detectable only on PAS stain or electron microscopy) to severe (readily visible on standard light microscopy).

pattern: Infant activity during preductal oxygen saturation measurement describes how active the infant was during the measurement. If an infant is crying or in a deep sleep, the oxygen saturation measurement may not be accurate; therefore, knowing the infant's state is important for interpreting the saturation result.
replace_with: Records the infant's activity state during preductal oxygen saturation measurement, as crying or deep sleep may affect the accuracy of the reading.

pattern: Carnitine.free+Acetylcarnitine+Propionylcarnitine+Palmitoylcarnitine+Oleoylcarnitine+Stearoylcarnitine/Citrulline is a primary marker for Carnitine uptake defect, Maternal carnitine uptake defect. Secondary marker for Maternal glutaric acidemia type I and Maternal 3-Methylcrotonyl-CoA carboxylase deficiency.
replace_with: Newborn screening marker ratio of combined free carnitine, acylcarnitines, and stearoylcarnitine to citrulline, used to detect carnitine uptake defect and related maternal metabolic conditions.

pattern: Isovolumic relaxation time (IVRT) measured using spectral doppler. IVRT is an interval in the cardiac cycle, from the aortic component of the second heart sound, that is, closure of the aortic valve, to onset of filling by opening of the mitral valve. It can be used as an indicator of diastolic dysfunction.
replace_with: Echocardiographic measurement of isovolumic relaxation time using spectral Doppler, representing the interval from aortic valve closure to mitral valve opening as an indicator of diastolic function.

pattern: Whether a pre-hospital neurologic impairment scale screen was documented by EMS personnel. Pre-hospital neurologic impairment scales are defined as published validated scales or those required by state EMS agencies (e.g., GCS for suspected hemorrhagic stroke or any stroke with impaired consciousness, Los Angeles Motor Scale, etc.).
replace_with: Indicates whether a pre-hospital neurological impairment scale screening was documented by EMS personnel, using published validated scales or state-required assessments for suspected stroke patients.

pattern: Isovolumic relaxation time (IVRT) measured using tissue doppler. IVRT is an interval in the cardiac cycle, from the aortic component of the second heart sound, that is, closure of the aortic valve, to onset of filling by opening of the mitral valve. It can be used as an indicator of diastolic dysfunction.
replace_with: Echocardiographic measurement of isovolumic relaxation time using tissue Doppler, representing the interval from aortic valve closure to mitral valve opening as an indicator of diastolic function.

pattern: the yes/no indicator that asks whether the participant had used another substance of abuse; this includes misuse of over-the-counter (OTC) medicines, such as dextromethorphan, triple Cs, caffeine, bath salts, and anabolic steroids as part of the Timeline Followback Method assessment, a calendar-based measure for assessing self-reported retrospective daily estimates of alcohol, tobacco and/or illicit substance use.
replace_with: Self-reported assessment of other substance abuse including misuse of over-the-counter medicines, caffeine, bath salts, and anabolic steroids, using a calendar-based retrospective recall method.

pattern: The descriptions as related to perivascular inflammation is focused around blood vessels, whereas diffuse inflammation also spreads across the muscle tissue. Inflammatory cells identified include lymphocytes, neutrophils, macrophages, and eosinophils. At times, microorganisms can be identified and should be mentioned.
replace_with: Histopathological description of inflammation type in biopsy or autopsy tissue, characterizing the pattern (perivascular versus diffuse), inflammatory cell types, and any identified microorganisms.

pattern: Indicates if dietary recommendations were made at discharge. Examples of possible diets the patient may be put on include the Therapeutic Lifestyle Changes (TLC) Diet (a low saturated fat, low cholesterol diet) and an antihypertensive diet, such as the Dietary Approaches to Stop Hypertension (DASH) diet.
replace_with: Indicates whether dietary recommendations, such as a low saturated fat diet or the DASH antihypertensive diet, were made at hospital discharge.

pattern: A measurement of kidney function typically estimated from the blood creatinine level in addition to other factors such as race, age, gender and weight, expressed as milliliters per minute. Normal values vary depending on age, sex, and body weight but it is typically > 80 milliliters per minute (mL/min).
replace_with: Measurement of creatinine clearance as an estimate of kidney function, typically calculated from blood creatinine level along with demographic factors, expressed in milliliters per minute.

pattern: Isovolumetric contraction time measured using spectral doppler. Isovolumetric contraction is a term used in cardiac physiology to refer to an event occurring in early systole, during which the ventricles contract with no corresponding volume change. Record the time it takes for the contraction to occur.
replace_with: Echocardiographic measurement of isovolumic contraction time using spectral Doppler, capturing the early systolic interval during which the ventricles contract without volume change.

pattern: The patient's documented sex (gender) on arrival at the hospital. This term was created for the "Patient gender" data element on the Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality () Healthcare Event Reporting Form (HERF), version 1.2. [https://ushik.ahrq.gov/ViewItemDetails?&system=mdr&itemKey=169064000&enableAsynchronousLoading=true]
replace_with: Documents the patient's sex or gender on arrival at the hospital.

pattern: Documents any use of any type of ventilatory assistance used to sustain respiration on the date of final inpatient discharge (the date of discharge from the last inpatient rehabilitation hospital or discharge from the last acute care hospital if the patient is not admitted to a rehabilitation hospital).
replace_with: Indicates whether any type of ventilatory assistance was being used to sustain respiration on the date of final inpatient discharge from an acute care or rehabilitation hospital.

pattern: The intended outcome(s) to be achieved via the prescription or administration of an antithrombotic treatment. *If the treatment goal is not available in the selections for this data element, code "Other" and record the treatment goal in the free-text data element "Antithrombotics Treatment Goal - Other".
replace_with: Records the intended therapeutic outcome for prescribing or administering antithrombotic treatment.

pattern: This term is intended to encode test results obtained within minutes. An example is the bioLytical INSTI HIV-1 kit, which can be used on serum, plasma, or whole blood, including fingerstick. At the time of creation, only the HIV-1 test is approved for use in the US, although an HIV-2 test is available.
replace_with: Rapid immunoassay for detecting HIV-1 antibodies in serum, plasma, or blood, including fingerstick specimens, providing results within minutes.

pattern: This term was created for the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) 2015 Edition Health Information Technology (Health IT) Certification Criteria final rule. [https://www.federalregister.gov/articles/2015/10/16/2015-25597/2015-edition-health-information-technology-health-it-certification-criteria-2015-edition-base]
replace_with: Records the sex assigned to an individual at birth, as defined by health information technology certification criteria.

pattern: The intended outcome(s) to be achieved via the prescription or administration of a thrombolytic treatment. *If the treatment goal is not available in one of the selections for this data element, code "Other," and code the free-text treatment goal in the data element "Thrombolytic Treatment Goal - Other".
